questions.question: How do humans adapt to their environment?
questions.correct_answer: By adjusting homes, clothing, and daily activities according to climate, terrain, and resources
questions.explanation: Adaptation helps humans survive and use available resources efficiently.

questions.question: How does environment influence housing and clothing?
questions.correct_answer: People build houses and wear clothes suited to climate and available materials
questions.explanation: Cold regions require insulated homes and woollen clothes; warm regions have light homes and cotton clothes.

questions.question: How does agriculture depend on the environment?
questions.correct_answer: Crop choice, farming methods, and irrigation depend on climate, soil, and water availability
questions.explanation: Wet regions grow rice, dry regions grow millet; terrace farming is used in hilly areas.

questions.question: How does geography affect trade and transportation?
questions.correct_answer: Rivers, mountains, and deserts determine trade routes and transport methods
questions.explanation: Merchants in mountains use mules; rivers serve as natural trade routes.

questions.question: How do natural disasters affect communities?
questions.correct_answer: Floods, droughts, cyclones, and earthquakes influence settlements and survival strategies
questions.explanation: Communities build elevated houses or irrigation systems to cope with disasters.

questions.question: What are some solutions to environmental challenges?
questions.correct_answer: Irrigation, terrace farming, reforestation, and disaster management
questions.explanation: These solutions help sustain communities despite water scarcity, soil erosion, and climate extremes.

questions.question: What do regional case studies show?
questions.correct_answer: Different regions show unique human adaptations based on environment, culture, and technology
questions.explanation: Desert farmers use rainwater harvesting; northeastern hills practice jhum cultivation.

questions.question: What is sustainable living in relation to environment?
questions.correct_answer: Using resources wisely to maintain harmony with nature and ensure future survival
questions.explanation: Traditional water tanks in South India collect rainwater for long-term use.

questions.question: How does geography shape human life?
questions.correct_answer: It influences settlement patterns, food habits, and occupations
questions.explanation: Civilizations developed along rivers for fertile land and water resources.

questions.question: How do humans modify the environment?
questions.correct_answer: Through irrigation, dams, urbanization, and deforestation to meet their needs
questions.explanation: The Bhakra Nangal dam altered local geography to provide water and electricity.

notes.title: Adaptation
notes.content: Humans change lifestyle, housing, and clothing to suit environment.

notes.title: Housing & Clothing
notes.content: Homes and clothes are designed for climate and local materials.

notes.title: Agriculture
notes.content: Crop selection and farming methods depend on environment.

notes.title: Trade & Transport
notes.content: Geography affects how goods are moved and trade routes formed.

notes.title: Disasters & Solutions
notes.content: Communities adopt strategies like raised houses and irrigation to cope with natural disasters.

facts.fact: Terrace farming prevents soil erosion in hilly areas.
Rainwater harvesting helps communities in water-scarce regions.
Rivers, mountains, and deserts shape trade and settlement patterns.
Humans both adapt to and modify the environment to meet their needs.
Sustainable practices ensure resources last for future generations.
